data.title: offering
data.description:
• American Critical Minerals (OTCPK:APCOF) plans to raise up to $1 million through a non-brokered private placement of up to 5 million units at $0.20 each. Each unit includes one common share and half a warrant, with full warrants exercisable at $0.30 for 24 months.
  • Proceeds will support development of the Green River Project, including exploration work for lithium and bromine, updating its technical report, paying concession and regulatory fees, marketing, and general working capital.

data.title: Sanofi falls after late-stage trial data for eczema therapy
data.description:
Sanofi (NASDAQ:SNY) ADRs fell ~10% in the premarket on Thursday after data from a late-stage trial for amlitelimab, its experimental therapy for the inflammatory skin condition atopic dermatitis (often referred to as eczema), disappointed investors.

The French drugmaker said that the global Phase 3 trial named COAST 1, which tested two dosing regimens of the monoclonal antibody, met all primary and key secondary endpoints. However, there will be questions over how the injectable will stack up against blockbuster eczema drugs, such as Dupixent, which Sanofi (NASDAQ:SNY) markets with Regeneron (REGN).

According to the company, amlitelimab dosed either every four weeks or every 12 weeks reached all primary and key secondary endpoints related to skin clearance and disease severity, with a statistically significant and clinically meaningful impact against placebo at Week 24.

COAST 1, one of five clinical trials supporting the company’s OCEANA development program for amlitelimab in AD, enrolled more than 600 patients aged 12 years and older with moderate-to-severe AD.

Regarding safety, the Paris-based drugmaker said treatment-emergent adverse events were more common in the placebo arm, and the rates of treatment discontinuations due to TEAEs were similar across the placebo arm and amlitelimab arms.

Other trials in the OCEANA program, namely SHORE, COAST 2, AQUA, and ESTUARY, which, along with COAST 1, will support global regulatory submissions for amlitelimab for AD, are expected to generate data through 2026.

data.title: Shoe Carnival gains after pointing to strong back-to-school results in August
data.description:
Shoe Carnival (NASDAQ:SCVL) shot up in early trading on Thursday after the retailer issued a mixed second quarter earnings report. 

Revenue fell 7.9% from the same quarter a year ago to $306.4 million. Comparable sales declined 7.5%, including a high single-digit decline at Shoe Carnival and break-even results at Shoe Station.

CEO Mark Worden noted that as Shoe Carnival (NASDAQ:SCVL) moved into the back-to-school period in early August, execution hit a higher level. "We delivered positive comparable store sales for the company and margin expansion across all banners during the period that drives approximately 25 percent of our annual profits. This return to growth during our highest-stakes season - ahead of our projected timeline - validates that our transformation is accelerating," he highlighted.

By banner, Shoe Station grew comparable sales high-single digits in fiscal August, driven by high single-digit growth in the children's category and low-twenties growth in the adult athletics category with margin expansion. Meanwhile, Shoe Carnival achieved positive children's category comparable sales with margin growth while maintaining discipline in other categories.

Shoe Carnival's (SCVL) gross profit margin rate increased 270 basis points to 38.8% of sales. The margin expansion was said to demonstrate the power of the company's rebanner strategy. Merchandise margin improved 390 basis points, driven by disciplined pricing across all banners, a favorable mix shift toward merchandise preferred by Shoe Station's higher-income customers, and strategic inventory investments. That improvement more than offset 120 basis points of deleverage in buying, distribution, and occupancy costs.

At the end of the quarter, Shoe Carnival (SCVL) operated 428 stores: 313 Shoe Carnival stores, 87 Shoe Station stores, and 28 Rogan's stores. The Shoe Station store count has more than doubled since the second quarter of 2024.

Looking ahead, Shoe Carnival (SCVL) expects full-year revenue of $1.12 billion to $1.15 billion (midpoint $1.135 billion) vs. $1.16 billion consensus. The company sees full-year EPS of $1.70 to $2.10 (midpoint $1.90) vs. a prior outlook for $1.60 to $2.10 and the consensus estimate of $1.79.

Shares of Shoe Carnival (SCVL) were up 7.4% in premarket action.

data.title: Rithm Capital to acquire Crestline Management
data.description:
• Rithm Capital (NYSE:RITM) has agreed to acquire Crestline Management, an alternative investment manager with approximately $17 billion in assets under management.
  • The transaction is expected to close in the fourth quarter 2025, subject to customary regulatory approvals and closing conditions.
  • Founded in 1997, Crestline is a fully integrated platform offering private credit and alternative investment strategies across direct lending, opportunistic credit, and fund liquidity solutions.
  • Upon closing of the transaction, Crestline’s investment team, committees, and strategies will remain unchanged, and the firm will maintain its existing offices in Fort Worth, New York, Toronto, Tokyo, and London.
  • The deal significantly strengthens Rithm’s asset management platform, bringing insurance and reinsurance capabilities to the platform.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Trump-linked American Bitcoin more than doubles mining capacity to 24 EH/s
data.description:
American Bitcoin (NASDAQ:ABTC) has acquired and energized ~14 exahash-per-second (EH/s) of installed hashrate across ~16,300 ASIC servers, it said on Thursday.

This brings American Bitcoin's total installed hashrate from ~10 EH/s to ~24 EH/s with an average fleet efficiency of ~16.4 joules per terahash (J/TH) as of September 1, 2025.

Shares in American Bitcoin (NASDAQ:ABTC), the bitcoin mining company backed by Eric and Donald Trump, Jr., soared as much as 100% in early trading on Wednesday, its first session after combining with Gryphon Digital Mining to make it a publicly traded company.

American Bitcoin (ABTC) is down 9% in premarket trading on Thursday.

"We are rapidly executing on a differentiated dual strategy to build Bitcoin-per-share," said Asher Genoot, Executive Chairman of American Bitcoin.

"In Q2 2025, our cost of revenue per Bitcoin mined was approximately 50% of our revenue per Bitcoin mined," Genoot added.

The purchased miners are deployed at the 205-megawatt Vega data center developed and operated by Hut 8 (NASDAQ:HUT), an integrated energy infrastructure platform and American Bitcoin's exclusive infrastructure provider.

data.title: 1-800 FLOWERS.COM Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.69, revenue of $336.6M

data.title: GitLab stock slides as outlook underwhelms, CFO exits
data.description:
GitLab (NASDAQ:GTLB) shares fell over 9% premarket on Thursday after a weaker-than-expected sales outlook for the third quarter, a conservative revenue guide for the full year, and a sudden CFO exit rattled investors.

For Q3, the company expects adjusted diluted earnings per share in the range of $0.19 to $0.20 on revenue of $238M to $239M. That compared with analysts estimates for earnings of $0.19 a share on revenue of $241.53M.

For full year of FY '26, the company raised its adjusted EPS outlook to $0.82–$0.83, up from its prior range of $0.74–$0.75 and ahead of the Street’s $0.75 estimate. It, however, reaffirmed net revenue guidance at $936M–$942M, versus consensus of $942.9M.

Analysts flagged that conservative forward guidance overshadowed solid Q2 results, amplifying concerns about second-half growth prospects. For the quarter ended Jul. 31, the company reported Q2 adjusted EPS of $0.24 on revenue of $236M (+29.2% Y/Y)—both topping Wall Street estimates.

Separately, the company said Brian Robins will step down as chief financial officer effective September 19, 2025, to pursue another opportunity. James Shen, vice president of finance, is expected to be named interim CFO.

data.title: P2 Gold announces $6M financing
data.description:
• P2 Gold (TSXV:PGLD:CA) completed a non-brokered private placement of up to 30 million units at a price of $0.20 per unit for gross proceeds of up to $6M.
  • The units to be issued under the offering will be offered to purchasers pursuant to the listed issuer financing.
  • Each unit will consist of one common share in the capital of the company and one common share purchase warrant.
  • The proceeds of the offering will be used to fund exploration and development expenditures at the Gabbs Project in Nevada and for general corporate purposes.

data.title: Spotlight on DraftKings, FanDuel, Robinhood, Kalshi, and Polymarket as NFL football arrives
data.description:
The NFL regular season kicks off on Thursday night with a high-profile matchup between the defending Super Bowl champions Philadelphia Eagles and Dallas Cowboys. The first game of the season also marks the beginning of the busiest time of year for sports betting.

The rise of prediction markets as an alternative to traditional sports betting in casinos or through apps is of high interest this year. Notably, Polymarket was given the green light to go live again in the U.S. just ahead of the NFL season. 

Meanwhile, Kalshi submitted a new type of contract with the Commodity Futures Trading Commission that includes multiple parts and payouts determined by multiplying the odds of each component of the bet. In its filing with the Commodity Futures Trading Commission, Kalshi defined contract "outcomes" as a set of specific achievements, results, or events specified that may include game outcomes, player statistics, team statistics, championships, awards, season statistics, playoff outcomes, draft outcomes, and milestones. That broad definition opens the door for contracts that resemble parlay or prop bets made on sports betting apps.

In another related development, Crypto.com and Underdog Sports are reportedly forming a partnership to launch sports prediction markets across 16 U.S. states. Robinhood Markets (NASDAQ:HOOD) is also in the mix after prediction markets for college and pro football.

While prediction markets are not yet considered a substantial threat to established sports betting operators, the companies do have the ability to operate in states that have not legalized sports betting, such as California, Texas, and Florida.

Americans are expected to place an estimated $30 billion in legal sports bets on the upcoming 2025 NFL season through U.S. sportsbooks, according to a forecast from the American Gaming Association. That tally would be an 8.5% increase over last season’s revised estimated handle of $27.6 billion on NFL games. The estimate includes preseason games, futures booked as early as March, the playoffs, and Super Bowl LX in February of 2026.

As of the 2025 NFL season kickoff, legal sports betting is available in 38 states plus Washington, D.C., and Puerto Rico. Missouri is the only new state approved for legal sports betting compared to a year ago, but its launch date of December 1 is well after the NFL season starts. The roughly direct comparison to a year ago means most of the growth in the U.S. sports betting market will be organic.

Stocks with ties to sports betting include DraftKings (NASDAQ:DKNG), Flutter Entertainment (NYSE:FLUT), MGM Resorts International (NYSE:MGM), Caesars Interactive (CZR), Rush Street Interactive (RSI), Wynn Resorts (WYNN), Penn Entertainment (PENN), Bally's Interactive (BALY), Sportradar (SRAD), Genius Sports (GENI), GAN Limited (GAN), Churchill Downs (CHDN), and Boyd Gaming (BYD). Privately owned Fanatics, Bet365, Circa Sports, and Hard Rock International are also active in sports betting in the U.S.
